pages.full_text: you could feel a breeze. Charles said 'It was big, but not as big as he thought it would be'. We need to go back in a couple of years. It showed lots of 'economic' fruit &
photo_captions: Looking through the Hemp fence towards the Biomes. The Eden Project, Bodelva, St Austell Cornwall PL24 25G
pages.notes: Text at top continues from a previous page; sentence ends with 'fruit &' and continues onto the right page. Page is dominated by a photograph of the Eden Project biomes viewed through a hemp/rope spiral fence structure. Photo caption written vertically beside the photo. The postcode reads either 'PL24 25G' or 'PL24 256' — difficult to distinguish. Right edge of page shows partial letters of text that continues on the right page.

pages.full_text:
veg :- 'wood' for indigo (which comes from different plants in different countries), hemp, cocoa, aloe, cassava, rice, banana. One zone was hot & humid like jungles, the other warm & temperate - the Med., S.A., Australia, California. Lovely planting of veg in arc-shaped rows.

I liked : serviceberry (rosaceae, amelanchier canadanis, i.e. saskatoon bush). gramineae poaceae, stipa gigantea (grass), himalayan honeysuckle (capri foliaceae, leycesteria formosa) hoary willow (salicaceae, salix elaeagnos), Scrophulariaceae (veronica peduncularis, georgia blue), blue ground cover, camellia theaceae, camolia salverensis (poor soil, heavy shade). wood (cruciferae brassicaceae, [?isatistinctoria].

We [struck:returned] [written above:preached] to the campsite by 4 + sunbatted on the green mat. John revised for his GCSEs, while the rest of us walked down to the estuary. To reach the campsite we had
pages.notes: Text continues from left page (beginning mid-sentence: 'veg :-'). The left edge of the page shows the vertical photo caption from the L page photo. The word 'returned' appears to be struck through and replaced with 'preached' written above it — this may be a deliberate humorous substitution or a correction. Several botanical Latin names are written; some are hard to read with certainty: 'canadanis' could be 'canadensis'; 'isatistinctoria' possibly 'isatis tinctoria' (woad). 'camolia salverensis' likely 'camellia x saluenensis'. 'leycesteria formosa' = himalayan honeysuckle. Page ends mid-sentence, continuing on a subsequent page. Year not determinable from this page alone; the Eden Project opened in 2001, so the visit is 2001–2003.
